checks.evidence: Pricing page header states 'Trust the n°1 CRM' and positions Twenty as open-source CRM. Intro docs describe it as a CRM platform for building custom solutions.
OAuth docs page explicitly documents both API Keys (Settings → API & Webhooks) and OAuth 2.0 (authorization code + PKCE and client credentials flows), including a comparison table.
Pricing page shows 'Start your free trial today without credit card.' API access is listed as 'Yes' on both Pro ($9/user/mo) and Organization ($19/user/mo) plans. Self-hosting is free (open-source).

API docs page documents REST (/rest/) and GraphQL (/graphql/) APIs for CRUD, Metadata API (/rest/metadata/ and /metadata/) for schema management, batch operations up to 60 records, webhooks (sidebar), and rate limits (100/min on API docs page; pricing page shows 50/min Pro, 100/min Org).
Pricing page under 'Developers' section explicitly lists 'MCP server — Yes' for both Pro and Organization plans with checkmarks.

All prerequisites confirmed: public REST/GraphQL API (docs), self-serve free trial without credit card (pricing), API key and OAuth authentication (oauth docs), MCP server available (pricing). No material blockers observed.
